title: An official wax seal pressed once into the governing material. The definition is the seal itself: applied at compile time, impossible to undo, permanently authoritative. The `->` operator at center is what the seal certifies — every valid transition in the governed domain. The radial tick marks are the rule endpoints: positions where authority terminates and nothing beyond them can be structurally reached.
conceptualBridge: The pressed seal means Precept's definition is irrevocably final because compile-time verification applies the governing mark once — after which the contract cannot be altered and every transition it certifies is permanently structurally valid.
decision: Kept at threshold (4.0): the wax-seal aesthetic is a genuinely new direction and the crimson palette is unused in the active set; the 8 tick marks hurt visual economy — next iteration should simplify to just the seal circle + `->` center mark.
